bids.reasoning: At the start of the negotiation (t=0), I aim to propose an offer close to my optimal utility. The opponent's initial offer is not aligned with my preferences, as it provides minimum utility across all issues. The message suggests a collaborative approach, but the numbers indicate otherwise. Given this, I will propose a bid that maximizes my utility by selecting my highest preference values to set a baseline for negotiations.

The opponent's offers have remained constant and minimal in terms of utility; they have consistently chosen the least preferred values for all issues. Given the early stage of negotiation and very low time pressure, I will maintain my position with an offer that maximizes my utility. My primary aim is to anchor my expectation strongly within the negotiation and potentially motivate the opponent to start moving their position. There are currently no signs of concession from the opponent, so I will reiterate my initial offer maximizing my utility.

At this early stage of negotiation (t=0.04), the opponent is offering low-utility options for all issues, consistently presenting offers with the lowest possible utility for me. Despite their messages claiming fairness and mutual gains, their bids suggest a firm stance. Given the time and the need to push for a better result, I will continue to propose high-utility offers, holding to the highest preference values. As time is not yet pressuring us significantly, there's room to maintain this position and negotiate assertively.

At this early stage of the negotiation (t=0.06), I aim to maximize my utility by sticking to my highest-preference bid of valueA across all issues. The opponent has not demonstrated any significant concession in their offers, remaining firm at low-utility values for me, particularly on issueA, which is my most critical issue. Given the high importance I place on issueA and issueB, I will not change my strategy yet, as early concessions could weaken my position unnecessarily.

At this early stage in the negotiation process, I aim to maintain my position and achieve the highest possible utility since there is still ample time for further negotiation. The opponent's offers have primarily involved lower utility values for me, with little indication of significant concession on their part to improve my utility. Their messages highlight mutual benefits, but the substantive offer analysis doesn't reflect that claim. I am therefore motivated to continue proposing an offer with maximal utility value for myself, to establish a strong initial negotiating point.

With the time still early in the negotiation stage (t=0.1), my focus is on maintaining offers that closely align with my highest utility values. As they seem intent on finding a middle ground and acknowledging their movement on "issueA," I should consider maintaining a firm stance on my preferred values to set the tone for future concessions while ensuring I remain distinct from my previous offers.

The opponent has moved slightly by adjusting the values for issues A and C, though only in small increments, while maintaining low-value positions in other issues which suggests they are still holding out for a more favorable deal. Despite the opponent's repeated assurances in the messages about fairness, their offers remain skewed to their advantage. As time is still early at t=0.12, I will maintain a high-utility offer by slightly conceding on issueA while keeping high utility values in other essential areas where my preference is strong.
